rulings.Case Title: ELVIRA GONZALEZ VS. SUNRUN INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Claims With Prejudice And Class Claims Without Prejudice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, December 30, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ELVIRA GONZALEZ's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff's Claims With Prejudice And Class Claims Without Prejudice. Plaintiff Elvira Gonzalez's Motion for Request for Dismissal of Individual Claims With Prejudice and Class Claims Without Prejudice to which no opposition has been filed is GRANTED. Plaintiff's individual claims are dismissed with prejudice. The class claims are dismissed without prejudice. (See California Rules of Court, Rule 3.770.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: LUIS E. MORELL VS. VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, December 30, 2024, Line 2, PLAINTIFF LUIS MORELL's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Continued to January 29, 2025. The opposing party shall provide a courtesy copy of the opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. (SF Local Rule 2.7B.) In addition, there is no record that Volkswagen actually filed the opposition with the court. The court only learned of it because of the reply brief. Immediately file the opposition. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ).
